title: Clean up test_config_schema.py: consolidate helpers, fix assertions, improve coverage
analysis:
This is a post-merge cleanup of test_config_schema.py from task 221 (YamlSettingsSource hardening). The file has six concrete issues: (1) _DummySettings(BaseSettings) defined identically at lines 182, 236, 306, 326 — extract to module level. (2) Inline imports of pydantic_settings.BaseSettings at lines 181, 234, 304, 324 and pydantic.fields.FieldInfo at line 333 — move to module-level import block. (3) pytest.raises(match=str(path)) at lines 246, 257, 272 passes raw path strings as regex; dots/hyphens in tmp_path are metacharacters — use re.escape(). (4) test_unreadable_file_raises_runtime_error (line 249) only skips Windows, not root where chmod(0o000) is a no-op. (5) Implementation uses 'raise ... from e' but only one test (line 288) verifies __cause__; corrupt YAML and unreadable file error tests don't. (6) test_utf8_yaml_loaded_correctly passes on any UTF-8 system regardless of the encoding parameter — needs mock-based verification.

The source under test is fused-memory/src/fused_memory/config/schema.py. YamlSettingsSource.__call__ (lines 60-73) has three error paths: YAML parse error (line 66-67), OS error (same except clause), and env-var expansion error (lines 70-73). All use 'raise RuntimeError(...) from e'. The open() call at line 64 uses encoding='utf-8' explicitly.

Approach: TDD order — write new/strengthened test assertions first (items 5, 6), verify they pass against existing implementation, then apply refactoring (items 1-4) that must not change behavior. Each step is atomic and independently committable.

prerequisites.description: Verify all existing tests in test_config_schema.py pass as baseline before any changes

steps.description: Add __cause__ assertion to test_corrupt_yaml_raises_runtime_error: capture exc_info and assert exc_info.value.__cause__ is not None (verifies 'raise ... from e' chaining for YAML parse errors). This strengthens the existing test at line 241.
Verify step-1 test passes — no implementation change needed since schema.py line 67 already uses 'raise RuntimeError(...) from e'. Run test suite to confirm green.

Add __cause__ assertion to test_unreadable_file_raises_runtime_error: capture exc_info and assert exc_info.value.__cause__ is not None (verifies chaining for OSError from permission denied). This strengthens the existing test at line 250.
Verify step-3 test passes — no implementation change needed since the same except clause chains with 'from e'. Run test suite to confirm green.

Write new test test_utf8_open_passes_encoding_kwarg in TestYamlSettingsSourceEncoding. Use unittest.mock.patch on 'builtins.open' to intercept the open() call and assert it was called with encoding='utf-8'. The mock should use a side_effect that calls the real open so the test still exercises YAML loading, then verify via mock.call_args that encoding='utf-8' was passed.
Verify step-5 test passes — no implementation change needed since schema.py line 64 already uses encoding='utf-8'. Run test suite to confirm green.

Move inline imports to module level: add 'from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings' and 'from pydantic.fields import FieldInfo' to the module-level import block (after the existing pydantic imports). Remove the 5 inline import statements at lines 181, 234, 304, 324, and 333. Run full test suite to verify no regressions.

Extract _DummySettings to module level: define a single 'class _DummySettings(BaseSettings): pass' after the import block. Extract a module-level helper '_make_source(path)' that creates YamlSettingsSource(_DummySettings, config_path=path). Replace the 4 inline _DummySettings definitions and the 2 identical _make_source methods (in TestYamlSettingsSourceErrorHandling and TestYamlSettingsSourceEncoding) with calls to the module-level helper. Update the 2 setup_method methods (TestYamlSettingsSourceEnvVarExpansion and TestYamlSettingsSourceABCContract) to use the module-level _DummySettings directly. Run full test suite to verify no regressions.

Use re.escape() in pytest.raises match arguments: add 'import re' to the import block (already imported by schema.py but not by tests). Replace 'match=str(bad_file)' with 'match=re.escape(str(bad_file))' at line 246. Replace 'match=str(locked_file)' with 'match=re.escape(str(locked_file))' at line 257. Replace 'match=str(config_file)' with 'match=re.escape(str(config_file))' at line 272. Run full test suite to verify no regressions.

Add root-user skip to test_unreadable_file_raises_runtime_error: add a second @pytest.mark.skipif decorator — 'pytest.mark.skipif(os.getuid() == 0, reason="root ignores file permissions")'. Place it alongside the existing Windows skip. Verify test still passes on non-root, non-Windows systems.

design_decisions.decision: Extract _DummySettings as a module-level class rather than a pytest fixture
design_decisions.rationale: A plain class is simpler and more explicit than a fixture for this use case. It's a trivial empty BaseSettings subclass with no parameterization or teardown needs. Module-level avoids fixture injection boilerplate while keeping the class definition in a single place.

design_decisions.decision: Extract _make_source as a module-level function rather than a shared fixture
design_decisions.rationale: The helper takes a path argument and returns a YamlSettingsSource — a pure factory with no test-infrastructure coupling. A module-level function is more Pythonic for this pattern. The two test classes that had identical _make_source methods (ErrorHandling, Encoding) can call it directly, and the two setup_method classes can construct the source inline using the shared _DummySettings class.

design_decisions.decision: Use side_effect wrapping real open() for UTF-8 encoding test rather than a pure mock
design_decisions.rationale: A pure mock of builtins.open would break YAML loading since yaml.safe_load needs actual file content. Using side_effect that delegates to the real open() lets us both verify the encoding kwarg AND exercise the full YAML loading path, making the test more robust.

design_decisions.decision: Add re import to test file even though re is already imported in schema.py
design_decisions.rationale: Test files should be self-contained in their imports. Relying on transitive imports through the module under test is fragile. Adding 'import re' to the test file's import block is explicit and correct.

design_decisions.decision: Perform refactoring steps (items 1-4) after new test assertions (items 5-6) are green
design_decisions.rationale: TDD discipline: first strengthen the test suite with new assertions (exception chaining, encoding verification), verify they pass, then refactor the test code structure. This ensures refactoring doesn't accidentally break the newly-added behavior.

design_decisions.decision: Use os.getuid() == 0 check rather than platform-specific root detection
design_decisions.rationale: os.getuid() is the standard POSIX way to detect root. The test already skips Windows (where getuid doesn't exist), so the POSIX-only function is safe. No need for more complex detection like checking capabilities.

reuse.how: Already autouse — all tests in the file automatically get CONFIG_PATH isolation. No changes needed.
These are identical — extract to module-level _make_source(path) function that both classes (and new tests) call.
Follow the same exc_info capture pattern when adding __cause__ assertions to corrupt YAML and unreadable file tests.
Constructor takes (settings_cls, config_path=None). The _make_source helper wraps this with _DummySettings as the settings_cls.
